title: Direct Response Marketing Copywriter – $2,000–$4,000/month
description:
Work Hours: Full-time, Mon–Fri, day shift



ABOUT US



Young Publisher | 



- We’re an international digital publishing company

- We’ve been hiring Filipinos for over 5 years

- We offer long-term roles

- We don't disappear, we pay on time



ABOUT THE ROLE



We’re looking for an experienced Direct-Response Copywriter who can:



- Take full ownership of performance-driven campaigns

- Write
s that get opened, read, and clicked

- Create stop-scrolling ads

- Craft persuasive copy that drives readers to act



This is a full-time role with clear growth opportunities. You’ll start with
s and ads, and as you prove yourself, move into sales pages and VSLs.



RESPONSIBILITIES



- Write broadcast
s and automated sequences

- Create Meta ad copy and video scripts

- Help produce ebooks and lead magnets using AI tools

- Progress into sales pages and VSL scripts for launches



BENEFITS



- Get direct feedback from an 8-figure copywriter: a serious opportunity to sharpen your skills fast.

- Get access to premium courses and AI communities. We invest in our team because we want long-term growth.

- We encourage AI tools — but only with strong critical thinking. This is not a beginner role.



ROLE SUMMARY



- Full-time (Mon–Fri, 8 AM - 5 PM PH Time)

- Long-term position with growth into sales pages and VSLs

- $2,000 – $4,000 USD per month, depending on experience

- Fully remote

- Reliable, on-time payments every two weeks via Wise



REQUIREMENTS



- At least 2 years of direct-response copywriting experience (not blog or content writing)

- Proven results: open rates, CTR, conversions, ROAS

- Experience writing for coaches, course creators, or info product businesses

- Native-level English for US, UK, or Australian audiences

- Strong understanding of hooks, emotional triggers, objection handling, and CTAs

- Experience with Meta ad copy and video scripts

- Ability to adapt to different brand voices

- Comfortable using AI tools with critical thinking

- Strong communication skills and independence

- Reliable, responsive, proactive, and coachable



HIRING PROCESS



#1: Paid test task

#2: Interview

#3: Paid test month at 50% salary



We’re looking for professionals who know their craft and want long-term growth.



HOW TO APPLY



Apply via our Typeform here: 
/to/CZb9go1y



Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.



Young Publisher

title: Youtube Long Form Editor
description:
YouTube Long-Form Video Editor (Storytelling + Retention Focused)

???? About the Role



We’re looking for a high-level YouTube video editor who understands storytelling, retention, and audience psychology — not just cutting clips.



You’ll take raw footage and turn it into engaging, high-retention YouTube videos that educate, inspire, and build a personal brand.



Our content focuses on:



Airbnb / Property Business

Entrepreneurship & Mindset

Faith & Personal Development

???? Responsibilities

Edit 1–2 long-form YouTube videos per week (8–20 minutes)

Structure content for maximum engagement and watch time

Add:

Clean jump cuts

Pattern interrupts (zooms, text, b-roll, sound effects)

Subtle, modern transitions

Insert relevant b-roll to support storytelling

Add captions for key 
nts

Improve pacing and remove unnecessary fluff

Support overall story flow (hook ? value ? payoff ? CTA)

???? Who We’re Looking For



You MUST:



Understand YouTube retention and viewer psychology

Know how to create a strong hook in the first 5–30 seconds

Be able to edit with intention, not randomly

Have experience editing long-form YouTube content



We are NOT looking for:

? Basic editors who just cut clips

? People who need constant direction

? Slow communicators



We ARE looking for:

? Someone proactive with ideas

? Someone who studies YouTube and content trends

? Someone who can improve the content, not just edit it



???? Editing Style Reference



We like styles similar to:



Ali Abdaal

Iman Gadzhi

Alex Hormozi



Clean. Engaging. Value-driven. Retention-focused.



???? Requirements

Experience with Adobe Premiere Pro / Final Cut / DaVinci Resolve

Strong understanding of pacing, sound design, and storytelling

Ability to source and use b-roll effectively



Bonus:



Thumbnail design

Understanding of YouTube SEO

Experience in business / educational content

? Workflow & Turnaround

Turnaround time: 48–72 hours per video

Open to feedback and revisions

Clear and consistent communication

???? Compensation

Per video or monthly retainer (based on experience)

Long-term opportunity as the brand grows

???? Test Task (Shortlisted Candidates Only)



You will be given a 3–5 minute raw clip to edit into a high-retention video.



We are testing:



Your editing quality

Your storytelling ability

Your understanding of retention



You will also be asked to briefly explain your decisions.



???? How to Apply (READ CAREFULLY)



To be considered, you MUST include:



Links to your best long-form YouTube edits

Examples of work you have done for clients (not just personal projects)

A short message explaining:

How you improve retention in videos

Your editing process

Your favourite YouTube channel and WHY

Start your application with the word: GROWTH

???? Applications Will Be Ignored If:

No client work is shown

No explanation is provided

Application looks generic or copy-pasted

???? Why Join Us?

Work with a fast-growing personal brand

Create content that impacts lives (business, mindset, faith)

Opportunity to grow into a lead editor role

title: Test Analyst - Remote
description:
We are seeking a detail-focused Test Analyst to support the quality and reliability of our core business systems. In this role, you will help ensure that enterprise applications function as expected by applying structured testing approaches across multiple stages of the development lifecycle.



The ideal candidate brings a solid IT foundation, strong knowledge of both the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and software testing lifecycle (STLC), and the ability to analyze systems thoroughly to identify potential issues before release.



Key Responsibilities:

- Test Planning and Preparation

- Collaborate with business analysts and developers to define testing strategies

- Develop detailed test plans and test cases for new implementations and system enhancements

- Test Execution and Defect Management

- Perform testing across multiple phases including system, integration, regression, and UAT

- Record and track defects, ensuring issues are resolved before release

- Provide regular updates on testing progress and results

- Enterprise System Validation

- Perform thorough testing of ERP systems and related business workflows

- Integration and Data Testing

- Verify data exchanges and message accuracy when testing EDI transactions and API endpoints

- Database Validation

- Use SQL queries to validate backend data and confirm database accuracy

- Continuous Improvement

- Recommend improvements to testing practices, coverage, and overall efficiency



Required Qualifications:

- Minimum 3 years of experience in software testing

- ISTQB Foundation Level certification or higher

- Strong knowledge of system, integration, and regression testing approaches

- Experience testing ERP platforms and EDI integrations/message formats

- Practical experience performing API testing

- Ability to write and execute SQL queries for backend validation

- Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills



Preferred Experience:

- Experience conducting performance testing, including load, stress, or volume testing

- Experience creating and maintaining test automation scripts using Java



Work Schedule :

6:30 AM - 3:00 PM Philippines Time

title: Outreach & Lead Generation Specialist
description:
We are in search of a performance-oriented Outreach & Lead Generation Specialist to help expand our outbound initiatives. This is a fully remote position with a strong emphasis on LinkedIn and
 engagement, along with contributions to content creation and basic marketing support tasks.



We’re looking for someone experienced in developing outbound pipelines and connecting with prospects through thoughtful, structured, and customized outreach strategies.



Role Responsibilities:



LinkedIn Prospecting & Outreach (Primary Responsibility):

-Initiate up to 400 targeted connection requests each month

-Identify and reach out to key stakeholders such as HR Directors, Managing Directors, and Sales Directors

-Manage personalized, multi-step follow-up sequences

-Utilize voice notes in follow-ups to improve response rates

-Analyze campaign metrics and refine messaging for better outcomes



 Campaigns & Lead Generation:

-Plan and execute cold and warm
 outreach campaigns

-Gather and compile prospect contact information through sourcing and data scraping

-Implement structured three-step
 sequences

-Sync LinkedIn-generated leads with
 outreach efforts

-Monitor campaign performance and enhance engagement and conversion rates



Content Creation Support (Secondary Focus):

-Prepare and schedule up to five LinkedIn posts weekly

-Develop written content based on provided videos and key messaging themes

-Content topics may include:

      Founder story

      Emotional intelligence (EQ-i 2.0)

      Cost of a bad hire

      Industry insights

-Maintain regular posting across both personal and company LinkedIn accounts



Campaign & Lead Magnet Assistance:

-Support the rollout of lead magnets, including AI-based assessments

-Contribute to campaign refinements based on engagement data and results



Marketing & Operational Support:

-Assist with basic SEO enhancements

-Help maintain CRM data and ensure proper website integration

-Use outreach and tracking tools to support campaign performance and organization



Required Qualifications:

-Minimum of 2 years’ experience in B2B outreach, lead generation, or an SDR role

-Strong English communication skills, with the ability to clearly express technical concepts

-At least 2 years of hands-on experience running LinkedIn outreach campaigns, including connection requests, follow-ups, and engagement using tools like LinkedIn Sales Navigator (built on LinkedIn)

-At least 2 years of experience managing cold
 outreach and sequencing using platforms such as Instantly or Lemlist (built on SMTP/email infrastructure)

-At least 2 years of experience in lead sourcing and data scraping using tools like Apollo (built on cloud-based SaaS data systems)

-Demonstrated ability to build and improve outbound pipelines, including campaign tracking, A/B testing, and conversion optimization (typically using CRM and analytics dashboards)

-At least 1 year of experience handling CRM systems and maintaining lead tracking workflows, ensuring proper integration across outreach tools and databases



Nice-To-Have but not required: 

-At least 1 year of experience supporting outreach within recruitment or B2B services environments

-At least 1 year of experience creating and scheduling LinkedIn content, including personal branding and company page management (via LinkedIn tools or schedulers)

-Basic understanding of SEO practices, including keyword usage and on-page optimization (typically applied through CMS platforms)

-Familiarity with AI-based lead magnets and campaign funnels, including assessment tools and gated content delivery

-Experience working with UK-based markets, including managing time zone alignment and communication expectations

-Exposure to marketing automation and campaign tracking systems connected to outreach workflows

-Experience using voice notes or multimedia approaches to improve prospect engagement

title: Family Governance Advisor (Remote)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW



We’re looking for a highly organized, thoughtful, and systems-driven Family Governance Advisor to help bring structure, alignment, and accountability to our family office operations. This part-time remote role is ideal for someone who thrives on creating clarity, improving communication, and building sustainable systems that support long-term legacy, leadership, and wealth stewardship.





Key Responsibilities:

• Develop and maintain family governance structures, including roles, responsibilities, and decision-making frameworks.

• Create and organize agendas for family meetings, board meetings, and leadership sessions.

• Track action items, follow-ups, and ensure accountability across members.

• Assist in drafting and organizing key governance documents (bylaws, policies, procedures, etc.).

• Support communication across family members to ensure clarity and alignment.

• Help plan and coordinate family meetings, retreats, and strategic sessions.

• Maintain organized records of decisions, meeting notes, and historical documentation.

• Assist in onboarding new members into the family office structure and processes.

• Identify gaps in communication, structure, or execution and recommend solutions.

• Work closely with leadership to ensure governance aligns with long-term vision and strategy.

• Utilize AI tools to streamline documentation, communication, and workflow processes.



Requirements:

• Strong organizational and project management skills.

• Experience with governance, operations, or administrative coordination (family office experience is a plus).

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to manage sensitive information with discretion and professionalism.

• Strong attention to detail and follow-through.

• Comfortable working with multiple stakeholders and personalities.

• Proactive, solution-oriented, and highly dependable.

• Comfortable adopting and using AI-powered tools.



Compensation:

• $5.18/hr, capped at $400/month.

• Option for pay increases tied directly to productivity, effectiveness, and impact.



This isn’t just an administrative role—it’s an opportunity to help build the structure and systems that support long-term family legacy, leadership, and wealth. If you can bring clarity, organization, and alignment to complex environments, this is your role.



To Apply for the job:

Send a message to 
 or simply reply HERE with a few bullet points on why you would like this position.

Change the subject line to “I Want to Work for You In [Insert the name of the city where Jeff Bezos was born] - Family Governance Advisor”

Thank you!

mbers to ensure financial systems and reporting are accurate and up to date.

Our work involves coordinating events, employees, vendors, and logistics. We are looking for a highly organized Executive Assistant who can help keep everything running smoothly.



This is a long-term opportunity with room to grow into a full time leadership role.





The Role



You will help organize the operations of several small businesses by managing schedules, coordinating future staff members, organizing information, and helping build systems that keep everything running efficiently.



This is not a simple task job. We are looking for someone who enjoys creating order, improving processes, and keeping teams organized.





Key Responsibilities



Operations & Organization

 • Maintain organized systems for our businesses

 • Track tasks, deadlines, and projects

 • Create and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

title: Amazon Virtual Assistant
description:
JOB OVERVIEW

We are looking for a highly skilled and reliable Amazon Virtual Assistant with solid experience in FBA, Amazon Seller Central management, product sourcing and account management. The ideal candidate will be great at communication, detail-oriented, and able to work well with minimum supervision.



Responsibilities:

Create and manage product listings in Amazon Seller Central

Conduct product research for profitable wholesale products

Contact and open accounts with brand owners, distributors, and wholesalers.

Ungating categories/brands when required

Analyse products using tools such as Keepa, SellerAmp (SAS), Helium 10, etc. to determine product viability (sales rank, competition, cost, ROI, profit margin).

Prepare and manage FBA shipments

Monitor inventory, pricing, stock levels and restock planning

Create and manage purchase orders.

Manage Seller Central tasks (listings, uploads, case logs, etc.)

Maintain spreadsheets and reporting

Communicate professionally with suppliers and internal team



Requirements:

Proven experience as an Amazon VA 

Strong knowledge of Amazon Seller Central

Strong understanding of tools such as SellerAmp, Keepa, Helium 10, Jungle Scout, etc.

Good English communication skills 

Excellent communication and organizational skills

Able to work independently and meet deadlines

Able to analyse data and make profitable sourcing decisions 

High attention to detail with data-driven decision-making

Reliable internet connection and availability for regular updates

Fast learner, reliable, self-motivated, and able to work with minimal supervision



Work Schedule 



- Part-time: 16 hours - 4 days per week

- Work hours: Flexible - Ideally 10:00am - 2:00pm UK time (6pm - 10pm in PH)

- Competitive salary in PHP (to be discussed during the interview, based on experience)





How to Apply:



Please submit the following:



1) Updated Resume

2) Brief voice/video introduction of yourself (BIG PLUS but not compulsory)

3) Your Amazon Wholesale FBA experience

4) Tools you’ve used for product research

5) Examples of tasks you’ve handled 

6) Include the words ‘Aim For The Stars’ in the
 subject so we know you read the full add. If you fail to do so, we’ll ignore your application.



 Subject Line: Amazon Product Research VA – Your Name

Successful applicants will be invited for an interview.

If you pass the interview, there will be a 1 month trial where you will be going through a paid evaluation before a formal permanent job offer is made based on your performance.



This role has the potential to become full-time if you perform well.

title: Operations & Administrative Coordinator
description:
Operations & Administrative Coordinator 

Independent Contractor (Remote) 



Engagement Type: Independent Contractor

Time Commitment: Full-time workload (approximately 40 hours per week)

Work Model: Fully Remote

Start Date: As soon as possible

Reports To: Founder & CEO



This role requires a full-time level of engagement, with an expected workload of approximately 40 hours per week. The contractor should maintain consistent availability during working hours to support daily operations and communication.

Work progress and task completion will be monitored through operational trackers, daily work plans, and end-of-day summaries.

About Skyang Bio

Skyang Bio is a U.S.-based biotechnology services company providing gene modulation and functional genomics solutions, including CRISPR libraries, ORF/cDNA clones, lentiviral packaging, and custom screening platforms. We support academic labs, core facilities, and biotech/pharma research teams across the U.S., Europe, and Asia.

Role Overview

This role supports the day-to-day administrative and operational activities of the company, ensuring internal workflows, communication, and tracking systems run efficiently.

The position focuses on maintaining operational organization, supporting executive coordination, and ensuring accurate tracking of business activities.

This is a remote, operations-focused role requiring strong organization, responsiveness, and accountability.



The role also provides support for internal sales coordination and lead generation activities to help maintain a healthy business pipeline.

Key Responsibilities

Operations & Administrative Support (Primary Focus)

• Support daily operational coordination and administrative activities

• Assist with scheduling and coordination of meetings and calendar management

• Prepare and send customer invoices based on completed services or project milestones

• Maintain accurate invoice records and track payment status from clients

• Follow up on outstanding or overdue invoices when necessary

• Maintain accurate internal tracking systems for:

o customer orders

o project status

o operational tasks

• Maintain and organize internal documentation and shared folders

• Assist with developing and maintaining internal standard operating procedures (SOPs)

• Provide regular operational updates and summaries to leadership

• Identify opportunities to improve internal workflows and operational efficiency

• Ensure internal tracking systems remain accurate and up to date

Invoice preparation, payment tracking, and overdue invoice follow-up are expected to be maintained accurately in the company’s operational and accounting records.

Internal Sales & Lead Generation Support (Secondary Focus)

• Support execution of Skyang Bio’s lead generation and outreach initiatives

• Follow and help improve Skyang Bio’s lead generation and
 engagement SOPs

• Research and compile qualified lead lists aligned with Skyang Bio’s services and target customers

• Execute outreach campaigns using approved
 templates and messaging

• Track outreach activities, responses, and follow-ups using internal tracking systems

• Maintain accurate records of leads, engagement status, and follow-up actions

• Achieve defined weekly
 outreach and engagement targets

• Monitor outreach performance and suggest improvements to
 engagement strategies

• Identify and flag warm or interested leads for internal follow-up

• Support communication and coordination with Skyang Bio distributors and external partners

• Maintain organized records of distributor contacts and communications

Outreach activity, engagement metrics, and follow-up actions are expected to be tracked and reported regularly.

Remote Work Expectations

Because this is a fully remote role, strong communication and responsiveness are essential.

The contractor is expected to:

• Monitor work
 throughout working hours

• Respond to internal and external
s within approximately one hour during working hours whenever possible

• Maintain proactive communication regarding progress and issues

• Notify leadership in advance if stepping away from work for more than 60 minutes during working hours

• Maintain organized and up-to-date operational records

• Actively manage tasks and responsibilities without requiring constant supervision

Daily Work Planning & Reporting

To maintain operational visibility in a remote environment, the role includes structured daily reporting.

Morning Work Plan

At the beginning of each workday, submit a short plan outlining:

• key priorities

• planned tasks

• any questions or items requiring guidance



End-of-Day Summary

Before the end of each workday, submit a short report including:

• tasks completed

• progress updates

• issues encountered

• questions or support needed

• priorities for the next day

Qualifications

• Experience in administrative support, operations coordination, or executive assistance

• Strong organizational and time management skills

• Excellent written communication skills

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

• Comfortable working with spreadsheets, trackers, and CRM tools

• Ability to work independently and manage responsibilities in a remote environment

• Strong attention to detail and accountability

Preferred:

• Experience supporting startup or small business operations

• Experience with CRM or lead generation systems

• Familiarity with
 outreach or sales support processes



Independent Contractor Relationship

This position is structured as an independent contractor engagement, not an employee position.

The contractor is responsible for managing their own schedule to meet the responsibilities and deliverables of the role.

Specific terms regarding work expectations, invoicing procedures, and payment schedules will be defined in a separate contractor agreement.



Compensation

Compensation for this role is $5 per hour.

This engagement begins with an initial evaluation period of approximately three weeks. During this period, the contractor will perform the responsibilities described in this role and demonstrate operational reliability, communication responsiveness, and the ability to manage tasks in a remote environment.

Continuation of the engagement after the evaluation period will depend on performance, including:

• responsiveness during working hours

• completion of assigned tasks

• accuracy of operational tracking

• adherence to workflow and reporting expectations

• demonstrating a proactive attitude in managing responsibilities and identifying improvements

• willingness to integrate into the company’s working culture and collaborate effectively with leadership

• openness to learning new skills and adapting to evolving operational needs



Application Instructions

To apply for this role, please submit:

1. Your resume or CV

2. A short paragraph describing your experience with administrative, operational, or remote coordination work

3. A brief explanation (2–3 sentences) describing how you organize and prioritize tasks when working remotely

To confirm that you have carefully read this job description, please include the phrase “Skyang Operations” in the subject line of your application
.

Applications that do not follow these instructions may not be reviewed.

mbers)

Final Note:

We move fast.

We value people who take initiative and get things done without needing constant guidance.

We are looking for long-term tea
mbers — consistency and reliability are extremely important to us.



We are actively hiring and looking to fill this position quickly.

 32–40 hours per week

The role focuses on managing projects, organizing operations, maintaining lead pipelines, and supporting partnership development.

This is not an entry-level role. The person in this position must be comfortable operating independently, communicating professionally, and maintaining organized systems that support company growth.

• Podcast guest appearances

title: Senior Business Development Manager (B2B Healthcare & NDIS) - ANZ Market
description:
Senior Business Development Manager (B2B Healthcare & NDIS) - ANZ Market

Location: Remote (Philippines)



Engagement Type: Full-Time Independent Contractor (40 hours per week, Australian Business Hours)



Company: CDM Direct



About Us CDM Direct is a leading provider of outsourced operational support and customer experience solutions. We are currently executing a major expansion across the Australia and New Zealand (ANZ) markets, specifically targeting the Aged Care and National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) sectors.



We provide high-value, tech-enabled operational support—including our flagship 'Rostering as a Service' (RaaS) solution powered by Zoom Contact Centre AI—to solve severe administrative, financial, and compliance bottlenecks for healthcare providers.



The Role This is not a scripted call centre role. This is a highly strategic, consultative B2B sales position working directly alongside our Australian leadership team.



As our market expansion lead, you will be responsible for driving new business acquisition across the ANZ health sector. You will:

• Conduct highly targeted, unscripted outreach to engage senior business decision-makers (CEOs, CFOs, Partners, and Operations Managers).

• Lead commercial conversations to uncover operational chaos, staffing bottlenecks, and financial pain points.

• Position CDM Direct’s RaaS and outsourced solutions with absolute clarity and commercial credibility.

• Secure "Qualified Meetings" (QMBs) for our senior sales executives, directly driving the top of the revenue pipeline.



The Ideal Candidate (The "Hunter") We are exclusively recruiting Tier 1 B2B sales talent. To succeed in this role, you must possess severe commercial resilience and a genuine "hunter" mentality. You will be dealing with busy executives in a highly regulated industry; you must be comfortable handling objections, navigating gatekeepers, and creating commercial urgency from scratch.



• Minimum 3+ years of proven success in outbound B2B sales, KPO, or SaaS business development.

• Exceptional, polished English communication skills capable of peer-to-peer conversations with Australian C-Suite executives.



• Deep discipline, self-management, and the resilience to execute daily strategic outreach in a remote environment.

• A stable, private home office with reliable high-speed internet.



What We Are NOT Looking For



• Entry-level customer service or call centre agents.

• Telemarketers who rely on rigid scripts.

• Candidates seeking a passive account management role; this is a proactive pipeline-generation position.



Remuneration & Commercial Benefits



We offer a highly lucrative, performance-driven commercial package designed to reward top performers:



• Base Retainer: PHP 85,000 per month.

• Health & Wellbeing: Up to PHP 50,000 annual allowance to fully reimburse the cost of a premium private HMO plan for you and one dependent.

• Year-End Bonus: An annual service bonus equivalent to one month's base fee (13th month), paid in December.

• Activity Bonus: NZ$50 for every Qualified Meeting Booked (QMB) to supplement your income while your pipeline builds.

• Revenue Commission: An uncapped 2.5% commission on the Year 1 Contract Value of closed deals, with an accelerator to 5% for high performance.



• Leave & Lifestyle: You will observe standard Australian public holidays as non-working days without any reduction to your base fee. After 12 months, you are permitted 10 days of planned unavailability per annum.



How to Apply



If you have the resilience and the commercial acumen to drive this expansion, please submit:

1. Your updated CV.

2. A link to your LinkedIn profile.

3. A brief cover letter outlining your most successful experience generating outbound B2B meetings with senior executives.

title: Paid Ads Specialist with Claude AI Systems or AI Integrated

title: Credentialing Specialist - Provider Enrollment (Florida, US Healthcare)
description:
We're building a modern, tech-forward healthcare organization and need a sharp, detail-oriented Credentialing Specialist to own our provider enrollment process end to end. This is an early hire on a small, high-impact team — you'll have real ownership and room to grow as we scale.



You'll be responsible for credentialing and enrolling our physicians, nurse practitioners, and physician assistants with commercial insurance payors and Medicare across the state of Florida. You'll manage the full credentialing lifecycle — from initial applications through re-credentialing and ongoing compliance — working directly in CAQH and individual payor portals.



This is not a "set it and forget it" role. We're growing fast, onboarding new providers regularly, and need someone who can build and maintain clean, reliable processes from the ground up.



---



The Role

You'll be responsible for credentialing and enrolling our physicians, nurse practitioners, and physician assistants with commercial insurance payors and Medicare across the state of Florida. You'll manage the full credentialing lifecycle — from initial applications through re-credentialing and ongoing compliance — working directly in CAQH and individual payor portals.

This is not a "set it and forget it" role. We're growing fast, onboarding new providers regularly, and need someone who can build and maintain clean, reliable processes from the ground up.



---



What You'll Do

- Submit and manage initial credentialing applications for primary care providers (MDs, DOs, NPs, PAs) with Florida commercial payors (BCBS, UHC, Aetna, Cigna, Humana, etc.) and Medicare

- Maintain and update provider profiles in CAQH ProView

- Enroll providers in PECOS (Medicare) and manage Florida Medicaid enrollment where needed

- Track and manage re-credentialing and revalidation timelines so nothing lapses

- Monitor and track provider licenses, DEA registrations, board certifications, malpractice insurance, and other expiring credentials

- Follow up on enrollment denials, deficiencies, and incomplete applications — resolve issues and resubmit

- Maintain organized records and status tracking for 10–30+ providers (and growing)

- Work within our internal platforms and tools to manage workflows — we are a tech-forward team and expect you to be comfortable adopting new systems quickly



What We're Looking For

Required:



- 3+ years of hands-on experience with US provider credentialing and payor enrollment 

- Direct experience with primary care provider types (physicians, NPs, PAs) — not behavioral health or nursing-only backgrounds

- Working knowledge of CAQH ProView, PECOS/NPPES, and major commercial payor portals

- Experience with Florida payors and/or Florida Medicare

- Ability to work US hours (9 AM – 5 PM PT or as agreed)

- Excellent English communication — written and verbal

- Highly organized, self-directed, and able to manage multiple provider files simultaneously without dropping balls



Strongly Preferred:

- AI fluency — you actively use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to work faster and smarter, and you're excited about integrating AI into your daily workflows

- Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments where processes aren't fully built yet

- Familiarity with primary source verification workflows

- Experience tracking credentialing status in spreadsheets, project management tools, or credentialing platforms



Why This Role

- Ownership: You'll own the credentialing function, not just execute tasks handed to you

- Growth: As Sapling Health grows, this role grows — potential to build and lead a credentialing team

- Modern team: We use AI-native tools and modern platforms. If you're tired of outdated workflows, you'll fit right in

- Competitive pay for the right candidate, with room to grow



How to Apply

Please include in your application:

- A brief summary of your credentialing experience — specifically which payor types and provider types you've worked with

- Whether you have Florida-specific payor enrollment experience

- How many providers you've managed credentialing for at one time

- Your comfort level with AI tools — do you use them regularly? Which ones?

- Confirmation that you can work US business hours

title: Freight Billing Specialist
description:
GH Logistics is a growing freight brokerage and asset based logistics company specializing in dry van and refrigerated freight. Built on disciplined systems, structured SOPs, and financial accuracy, we operate with a strong focus on execution, accountability, and real-time visibility.



As we scale nationally, we are hiring a Freight Billing Specialist to serve as a critical financial control point across both load validation and carrier invoicing workflows, ensuring loads and invoices are accurate, fully documented, dispute-free, and ready for Finance and factoring.



Role Overview

You will act as the final gatekeeper before loads and invoices move to Finance and factoring, ensuring every load is fully validated first, then properly invoiced and submitted. This role requires flexibility, ownership, and the ability to operate across both functions without compromising accuracy or SOP compliance.



What You’ll Be Doing



Load Validation 

• Validate all loads before final status (Completed, Cancelled, or TONU)

• Ensure every load reaches complete status (fully documented, financially accurate, dispute-free)

• Review and correct load statuses in TMS

• Enforce complete documentation (BOL, POD, white slips, rate confirmations, accessorials)

• Upload and verify documents in TMS and ELD systems

• Confirm financial accuracy (rates, TONU logic, FSC, accessorials, fines)

• Own and investigate carrier disputes before escalation

• Apply correct TONU vs Cancel determinations

• Maintain structured, audit ready notes on every load

• Prevent incomplete or inaccurate loads from reaching Finance

• Prepare validated loads for invoicing and factoring submission



Carrier Invoicing 

• Upload and process carrier invoices accurately in TMS

• Verify invoice amounts against rate confirmations and approved charges

• Ensure all required documents are complete (POD, BOL, detention, lumper, etc.)

• Request and follow up on missing or incomplete invoices

• Coordinate with carriers and factoring companies for documentation

• Identify discrepancies and escalate with proper support

• Submit validated loads to factoring companies per requirements

• Resolve Held, Rejected, and Denied invoices

• Maintain structured, audit-ready invoice records

• Prevent incomplete or unsupported invoices from reaching Finance



What Success Looks Like

• 95%+ of loads reach Finance without rework

• 0 missing documents (POD, white slips, etc.) at billing stage

• 0 financial discrepancies identified by Finance

• All loads and invoices are fully audit-ready

• Minimal disputes due to documentation or validation errors

• Loads and invoices processed within 24–48 hours

• Factoring submissions meet compliance standards



Required Skills & Qualifications

• Experience in US freight brokerage, load auditing, QA, carrier billing, or operations

• Strong understanding of dry van & refrigerated freight

• Knowledge of TONU, rate confirmations, accessorials, and FSC

• Familiarity with TMS, ELD, and tracking systems

• Experience with factoring companies (preferred)

• Strong documentation discipline and audit mindset

• High attention to financial accuracy and detail

• Ability to enforce SOPs without shortcuts

• Strong written and verbal English communication

• Minimum 45–50 WPM typing speed

• Comfortable working in a structured, accountability-driven environment

• Ability to shift priorities between validation and invoicing workflows



Work Setup & Schedule

• Full Time | 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (San Antonio time)

• Must work U.S. business hours

• Collaborative Zoom-based work environment (camera on)

• Time tracking tools used for accountability



Why Join GH Logistics?

• SOP-driven operational systems

• High impact financial control role across two critical workflows

• Direct involvement in revenue protection and billing accuracy

• Performance based growth opportunities

• Structured training and certification path



If you take pride in closing operational loops, enforcing standards, and ensuring financial accuracy from validation to invoicing, this role is designed for you.



Apply here:

 CRM Management & Optimization

Create, customize, and optimize sales pipelines, deal stages, and workflows.

Develop and implement automation strategies to improve lead management and sales efficiency.

 Sales Process & Enablement

Streamline and document sales processes to ensure consistency and effectiveness.

Work closely with sales teams to ensure they are leveraging HubSpot to its full potential.

Provide support for sales reps on HubSpot best practices and tools.

 Reporting & Analytics

Generate and analyze sales reports, dashboards, and KPIs to provide actionable insights.

Monitor pipeline health, sales cycle trends, and conversion rates to identify areas for improvement.

Assist in forecasting and performance tracking to support strategic decision-making.

 Cross-Functional Collaboration

Work closely with Marketing, Customer Success, and Finance teams to align sales operations with company objectives.

Ensure seamless integration between HubSpot and other tools (e.g.,
 automation, customer support platforms, etc.).

Support lead management and handoff processes between marketing and sales.

 Process Automation & Optimization

Identify bottlenecks in sales operations and implement automation solutions.

Maintain and improve lead routing, scoring, and segmentation processes.

Optimize
 sequences, templates, and communication workflows in HubSpot.

title: CRO Analyst / Conversion Strategist (Shopify DTC - Full Time)
description:
Company: Hoolest Performance Technologies (
)

Location: Remote (Philippines preferred, global considered)

Hours: ? FULL-TIME — 40 hours/week, Monday–Friday. This is not a part-time or project-based role. We require 2–3 hours of daily overlap during US business hours (MST). Remainder of hours are flexible.

Compensation: $1,000–$1,200 USD/month (full-time salary)

Payment: Weekly for first month, then biweekly. Via Wise, PayPal, or OnlineJobs.ph EasyPay.

Start Date: ASAP



About Us

Hoolest Performance Technologies (
) makes VeRelief — a handheld vagus nerve stimulator that calms your nervous system in under 60 seconds. It was built by a biomedical engineer, backed by peer-reviewed research, and it actually works. The VeRelief Prime ($199) is our flagship, the Hoolest Pro ($499) is the hands-free version, and we offer a gel refill subscription ($11/month or $99/year) that keeps customers coming back.

We've been around since 2017 and have 300+ real reviews. Combat veterans with PTSD say it stopped their panic attacks. Parents with chronic anxiety say they can finally sleep. People write to us saying this device gave them their life back. This isn't another generic DTC product — when you help more people find VeRelief, you're helping people who genuinely need it.

Our team is small, fast, and direct. We communicate daily on Slack, ship changes the same week we d
 on them, and don't waste time on corporate process. You'll work alongside a media buyer, an
 team, and operations — everyone knows each other by name and talks every day.

We need someone to own what happens after the ad click. That's you.

Before you apply, visit 
 and look at our VeRelief Prime product page. The best candidates will reference specific observations in their application.



The Role

You will be our first dedicated CRO Analyst. This is a full-time, long-term position — not a one-off project or part-time gig. Your job is to understand what is happening on our website — where visitors drop off, what confuses them, what convinces them — and turn those findings into clear, prioritized recommendations.

You are not expected to redesign our entire site. You are expected to watch, measure, analyze, and tell us exactly what needs to change and why. When changes need to be made, you will spec them clearly (wireframes in Figma, written briefs) so they can be built by our Website Builder (a separate hire) or by our internal team.

You are the eyes and the brain. Someone else is the hands.

Important context: You are not just optimizing for a single product purchase. Our business model includes the initial device sale ($199) AND a gel refill subscription ($11/month or $99/year). This means you are optimizing two conversion events: the initial purchase and the subscription attach rate. Understanding how these interact is part of the role.



What You Will Do



Review Clarity/Hotjar session recordings and heatmaps weekly (minimum 20 sessions/week)

Analyze Google Analytics (GA4) conversion funnels to identify where visitors drop off and why

Produce prioritized CRO findings reports with clear recommendations

Design A/B tests with proper hypotheses, success metrics, and sample size requirements

Create wireframes and copy briefs in Figma when page changes are needed

Write or direct on-site conversion copy (headlines, CTAs, objection handlers)

Sync weekly with our media buyer (our media buyer) to understand which ads and audiences are working, and translate that into on-site insights

Sync weekly with our
 manager to keep messaging consistent

Report wins, losses, learnings, and priorities to leadership every Friday

Help evaluate candidates for our Website Builder role





What You Will NOT Do



You will not be expected to build pages in Replo or Shopify (we have a separate hire for that)

You will not manage ads,
, or social media

You will not be treated as a general VA — this is a strategic, analytical role

This is an exclusive, full-time role. We expect you to be dedicated to Hoolest during working hours, not splitting time with other clients.





In Your First 30 Days



Complete a full CRO audit: 50+ Clarity session recordings reviewed across our main product pages

Map our Google Analytics (GA4) conversion funnels and document baseline metrics (CR, bounce, AOV, revenue per session, subscription attach rate)

Meet with our media buyer and our
 manager to understand what is working

Deliver a CRO Audit Report: your top 10 prioritized findings with recommended fixes, ordered by estimated impact



We are not asking you to change anything in 30 days. We are asking you to deeply understand our site and tell us what matters most.



In Your First 60–90 Days



Spec your highest-priority recommendations in Figma for our Builder to implement

Launch and manage your first 3–5 A/B tests

Establish a weekly CRO reporting cadence

Begin demonstrating measurable improvements on key pages

Help us identify and evaluate our Website Builder hire





Required Skills



Microsoft Clarity, Hotjar, or similar — You use session recordings and heatmaps as your primary tool, not occasionally

Google Analytics (GA4) — You can build funnels, read cohorts, and connect traffic source to conversion behavior

A/B testing — You design proper tests with hypotheses, run them correctly, and interpret results honestly

Figma — You can produce clear, annotated wireframes and page specs for a builder to implement

Shopify familiarity — You understand how Shopify pages, cart, and checkout work (you do not need to code)

English communication — You can write clear recommendations, copy briefs, and present findings without confusion



Nice-to-Have



Copywriting ability (you can write conversion copy, not just recommend it)

Experience with health, wellness, or high-AOV DTC products ($100+)

Understanding of paid media metrics (CPM, CTR, ROAS)

Experience with subscription-based DTC businesses (subscription attach rate optimization)

Experience with Replo, Shogun, or similar page builders





How We Work



Communication: Slack (daily), Loom (screen recordings and walkthroughs), Google Drive (documents and specs)

Tools we provide access to: Clarity, Google Analytics, Shopify, Replo, Figma (team plan)

You provide: Your own computer and reliable internet connection

Meeting cadence: Weekly sync with Luke/Nick (Fridays), weekly check-ins with our media buyer and
 manager, async communication throughout the week

Trial period: The first 30 days are a mutual trial period. If it is not a fit for either side, no hard feelings.





Why This Role Is Different

You are not a VA. You are an analyst who owns a critical business outcome. When conversion rate goes up, you did that. Your work directly moves revenue.

You will build a team. Within 60 days, you'll help us hire a Website Builder who reports to you. You're not staying a solo contributor — you're growing into a team lead.

One brand, deep focus. No agency chaos. No juggling 10 clients with conflicting brand guidelines. You will know our customer, our funnel, and our product deeply — and that depth is where the best CRO insights come from.

The work actually matters. We get messages from people saying VeRelief stopped their panic attacks, helped them sleep for the first time in months, gave them their life back after PTSD. When you improve a page and more people find this product — that's real.

We're a startup that acts like it. Fast decisions, direct communication, no layers of approval. If your data says we should test something, we test it this week. Leadership is in Slack every day. You'll never feel like you're shouting into a void.



How to Apply

Step 1: Send your resume and a link to your portfolio or case studies. We want to see examples of CRO work with actual data — not just design mockups. Show us a before/after with metrics.

Step 2: Answer these 3 questions in your application:



Tell me about a specific page you analyzed where you identified a conversion problem. What was the problem, how did you find it, and what did you recommend?

Visit 
/products/verelief-prime. Spend 5 minutes on the page. Name 2 things you think are working well and 2 things you would investigate first if you had access to Clarity data. (There is no wrong answer — we want to see how you think.)

Our product is a $199 device with a $11/month gel subscription attached at checkout. What unique CRO considerations does a device + subscription model present compared to a simple single-product purchase?



Step 3: Top candidates will receive a paid trial task (2–3 days, $100–$150 compensation regardless of outcome). You will review real session recordings from our site and deliver a prioritized findings report and an A/B test plan.

To filter for serious applicants, please begin your application with the word: CLARITY

We review every application personally. If your experience matches, you will hear from us within 5 business days.

title: Part-Time Parent Communication Assistant (Calls + Text)
description:
Job Description:



Hi, I run an online math tutoring program for middle school students in the U.S.



Parents reach out to us when their child is struggling, and I’m looking for someone who can respond quickly, follow up, and help them schedule a call with me.



This role involves a mix of text messaging and occasional calls depending on what the parent prefers. Many parents do not answer unknown calls, so text follow up is a big part of the role.



This is not a high pressure sales job. You are simply helping parents take the next step.



What you will do:



You will respond to new parent inquiries, send follow up messages if they don’t respond, and occasionally call leads. Most calls will not be answered, so follow up messaging is important.



You will ask simple questions to understand what is going on with the student and help schedule a call on my calendar.



Important:



You must sound natural, warm, and confident. You should not sound like a telemarketer or use overly scripted language.



We are not looking for aggressive salespeople or pushy communication.



Example message style:



Hey, I saw you reached out about math help. What’s been going on with your child lately?



Hours:



This is a part time role for about 7 to 10 hours per week. Work is done in short check ins throughout the day and must overlap with U.S. daytime hours.



Pay:



Hourly pay plus bonus for booked calls that show up.



To apply:



Please send a short voice recording introducing yourself and a sample message you would send to a parent looking for math help.

title: Graphic Designer/Content Creator For Wellness Marketing Agency
description:
Company: The Coupet Collective

About Us:

The Coupet Collective is a boutique digital marketing agency based in the U.S. that works exclusively with wellness brands — including compounding pharmacies, telehealth companies, medspas, fitness tech, supplement brands, and mental health platforms. We're a small, creative team that values high-quality design, clear communication, and taking real ownership of projects.



We're looking for a talented Graphic Designer/Content Creator to join us part-time and report directly to our CEO and Lead Brand & Content Strategist. This is a hands-on production role — you'll be executing design projects across multiple wellness clients, not managing strategy. If you love clean, modern design and thrive with clear creative direction, keep reading.



What You'll Be Doing:



- Designing branded social media graphics (Instagram,
, LinkedIn, TikTok), including static posts, carousels, stories, and short-form video cover images.

- Creating marketing collateral such as one-pagers, sell sheets, PDFs, flyers, and digital brochures

- Building and formatting presentation decks and client-facing documents

- Adapting and resizing assets across platforms and formats

- Laying out branded content using established brand guidelines, color palettes, and font systems

- Supporting content calendar production by designing batched assets on a weekly basis

- Light photo editing

- Organizing and maintaining design files in shared folders





What We're Looking For

Required:



- 2+ years of graphic design experience, ideally in a marketing or agency setting

- Strong proficiency in Canva Pro (this is our primary design tool — you must be advanced, not just familiar)

- Working knowledge of Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator) for more complex projects

- A polished portfolio that shows range — social media content, branded PDFs, marketing materials

- Clean, modern design sensibility (we lean minimal, editorial, and elevated — not cluttered or overly templated)

- Strong typography and layout skills

- Ability to follow brand guidelines precisely while still bringing creative ideas to the table

- Reliable internet connection and a distraction-free workspace

- Clear, responsive communication in English (written and verbal)

- Available to overlap with U.S. Pacific Time for check-ins and feedback loops



Nice to Have:



- Experience designing for wellness, health, beauty, or pharmaceutical brands

- Familiarity with Metricool, Later, or other social media scheduling platforms

- Basic video editing or motion graphics skills (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, After Effects)

- Understanding of social media design best practices (safe zones, aspect ratios, platform specs)





About the Role:

You'll report directly to our CEO and Lead Brand Strategist. This is not a strategy or account management role — it's a creative production position. We'll provide clear briefs, brand assets, and direction. Your job is to execute beautifully and efficiently.



What a typical week might look like:



- Receive a batch brief for a client's weekly social content (6–10 graphics)

- Design a branded PDF or one-pager for a new product launch

- Resize and adapt existing assets for different platforms

- Make revisions based on feedback (usually 1–2 rounds)

- Organize and upload final files to shared folders



We move fast, give clear feedback, and respect your time. If you do great work, this role will grow with us.



How to Apply

Please include the following in your application:



- A link to your design portfolio (Behance, personal site, or Google Drive folder — we need to see your work)

- 2–3 examples of social media graphics or marketing materials you've designed (bonus points for wellness or health-related work)

- Your experience level with Canva Pro — rate yourself 1–10 and tell us what you've built with it

- Your availability — hours per week and preferred working hours (include your time zone)

- Include the word "WELLNESS" at the top of your application so we know you read the full posting



Applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed.

title: Performance Video Editor (Ads + Promotional + Explainer Videos) – Long-Term Role
description:
Performance Video Editor (Ads + Promotional + Explainer Videos) – Long-Term Role



We are building a high-performance creative team focused on producing video content for our businesses and our clients across the U.S. and Canada.



This includes promotional videos, short-form ads 
, Instagra, TikTok, Reels, YouTube Shorts), and simple explanatory videos.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants stability, growth, and consistent work — not freelance gigs.



What you’ll be responsible for:

- Creating Editing short-form video ads (15–60 seconds) for platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube

- Create promotional and brand videos

- Produce simple explanatory / informational videos

- Add captions, hooks, sound effects, music, and pacing for engagement

- Turn raw footage into clean, high-converting video content

- Create multiple variations of videos (different hooks, formats, angles)



This role is about execution, speed, and understanding what makes videos engaging and effective.



Requirements:

- Strong experience editing short-form video content (TikTok, Reels, Shorts)

- Ability to create clean, modern, engaging videos

- Strong understanding of pacing, hooks, and viewer retention

- Ability to follow direction and also take initiative

- Fast turnaround and reliability

- Good English communication



Bonus (not required but valuable):

- Experience with
 / TikTok ads

- Understanding of direct response marketing

- Motion graphics or animation experience

- Experience creating UGC-style content



Important:

This is not a beginner role.

We are looking for someone who can figure things out quickly and execute efficiently.



You should already be comfortable editing without step-by-step guidance.



Compensation:

- Part-time: $400 – $900/month

- Full-time: $800 – $1,500/month

- Based on experience and performance

- Performance bonuses

- Paid time off (for long-term tea
mbers)



Work Setup:

- Part-time to start, with opportunity to move into full-time quickly

- Flexible working hours

- Daily overlap required: between 8:00 AM – 10:00 AM Eastern Time (ET), approximately 1 hour per day (exact time will be coordinated with the team)

- Remaining work can be completed on your own schedule



Growth:

We are building a team — not hiring freelancers.

Top performers will grow into senior and creative leadership roles.



Hiring Process:

- Short interview

- Paid test task ($15–$25 USD, fixed price) to evaluate real skills



The test task will be a small, real-world scenario relevant to the role and should take approximately 1–2 hours to complete.



To Apply (REQUIRED):



Please include ALL of the following:



1. Your experience with video editing (what types of videos have you created?)

2. 2–3 specific examples of your best work (ads, promotional, or explainer videos)

3. Which type of videos you are BEST at (ads, promo, explainer, etc.)

4. Write “VIDEO BUILDER” at the top of your application



Final Note:

We move fast.

We value people who take initiative and get things done without needing constant guidance.

We are looking for long-term tea
mbers — consistency and reliability are extremely important to us.

We are actively hiring and looking to fill this position quickly.

title: Email Marketing Manager (ECommerce) - Full Time, Remote
description:
We are looking for a strategic and performance-driven
 & SMS Marketing Manager to lead the charge in growing and optimizing our owned marketing channels. This role is key to delivering high-converting campaigns that drive customer engagement, retention, and revenue across our skincare and hair care product lines. You’ll turn customer insights into action, bringing value at every touchpoint while aligning messaging with product and brand goals.





Primary Duties and Responsibilities:

Own the strategy, execution, and optimization of all
 and SMS campaigns for multiple product lines (e.g., hair thinning shampoo, supplements, anti-aging SPF, and skin supplements).

Segment audiences and personalize messaging to maximize relevance, engagement, and conversion.

Develop and manage high-performing lifecycle flows including welcome, cart abandonment, post-purchase, and win-back sequences.

A/B test subject lines, content, offers, and creative assets to continuously improve key performance metrics (CTR, open rate, CVR, revenue per send).

Monitor and report on campaign performance; adjust strategy based on insights and trends.

Build surveys and gather customer feedback to inform future content and product ideas.

Collaborate with creative, product, and customer experience teams to ensure consistent and on-brand messaging.

Maintain a strong understanding of compliance and best practices in
/SMS marketing (e.g., CAN-SPAM, TCPA).

Suggest and implement new technologies, tools, and tactics to stay ahead of the curve.





Qualifications:

3+ years of experience in
 and SMS marketing, ideally in eCommerce or beauty/wellness.

Expert in Klaviyo or similar marketing automation platforms.

Strong writing skills and a keen eye for visual branding and user experience.

Solid understanding of customer behavior, segmentation, and marketing psychology.

Results-oriented with a deep passion for data, testing, and optimization.

Familiarity with tools like Rebuy or product recommendation engines is a plus.

Proactive, curious, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

Passion for skincare, wellness, and helping people feel their best.





What You’ll Help Shape:



Your role goes beyond sending campaigns—you’ll help us build smarter customer journeys by listening to our audience. You'll turn feedback into better product launches, sharper messaging, and a stronger brand voice. You’re part marketer, part strategist, part customer advocate.





Ready to Apply? Please answer the following questions:

 marketing (including writing the

About Us



Ennovation Brands, the parent company of Skin Research Institute (SRI), is dedicated to creating innovative, science-backed beauty and wellness products that deliver real results. With a strong and growing customer base, our mission is to provide high-quality solutions that enhance everyday lives. title: Industrial Designer (Brand & Product Form)
description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a highly creative Industrial Designer based in the Philippines to help define and evolve the visual identity of our product portfolio across high-end hair tools and power/home improvement tools.

This role focuses on product form, brand expression, CMF, and user experience. You will translate brand strategy into iconic, emotionally compelling physical products that stand out in competitive global markets.

From premium hair dryers to precision power tools, you will shape a cohesive and recognizable design language that defines who we are — while working closely with our product development team to ensure your vision is thoughtfully executed.



Responsibilities

• Lead the form development and aesthetic direction of new products across multiple categories.

• Create a distinctive and consistent design language that strengthens brand recognition.

• Explore concepts through sketching, form studies, ergonomic refinement, and 3D development.

• Develop thoughtful CMF (Color, Material, Finish) strategies aligned with brand positioning.

• Ensure designs consider real-world functionality, usability, and internal component integration.

• Collaborate closely with the product development team to align on feasibility, performance requirements, and manufacturing considerations while maintaining design integrity.

• Produce high-quality renderings and presentation visuals to communicate design intent.

• Conduct trend research and competitive analysis to inform forward-thinking design decisions.

• Contribute to a cohesive product ecosystem across SKUs and categories.



What We’re Looking For

• Strong portfolio demonstrating brand-led product design, form exploration, and CMF expertise.

• Experience designing consumer products (beauty, electronics, tools, home goods, lifestyle products, etc.).

• Exceptional understanding of proportion, ergonomics, surface development, and user interaction.

• Ability to balance aesthetics, functionality, and manufacturability.

• Strong sketching skills (digital or hand).

• Proficiency in 3D modeling and rendering tools such as Rhino, SolidWorks, Fusion 360, KeyShot, or similar.

• Clear communication skills and experience collaborating with cross-functional teams.



How to Apply

Please submit your portfolio showcasing concept sketches, form development, CMF exploration, and final product renderings.

We’re looking for designers who create products people recognize instantly — before they even see the logo.



What you'll get

Competitive Salary – Earn in USD!

Health Coverage (HMO) – Enjoy peace of mind after one year!

Paid Time Off – Because work-life balance matters!

title: B2B Appointment Setting Cold-Caller
description:
Job — B2B Appointment Setter — Telemarketing

? Are You a Top Performer? ?

Do you win sales contests? Are you consistently the top performer on your team? Do you receive praise and compliments from management? We want YOU, and we're willing to pay for it!



? About the Company ?

Join ITRoundTable, a B2B appointment setting leader with 25+ years of experience serving US tech clients. We're a fully remote company that promotes from within, fostering a happy culture where your hard work is recognized and rewarded.



? About the Role ?

We're looking for talented professionals with telemarketing or outbound calling experience to set up quality meetings for business prospects with our US clients who sell technology products and services. Whether you've worked as an Appointment Setter, Sales Representative, or other outbound calling role, your skills will be perfect for this position. You'll work from home with our supportive team on Google Meet/Chat, contacting business professionals in the US. This full-time remote position offers excellent pay for those with strong English skills who can work night-shift hours to align with US business hours.



? Job Highlights ?



• Type — Full-time position (40h/week)



• Location — Remote from the Philippines



• Hours — Night shift, Mon–Fri, aligned to 9–5 US Central (CST/CDT) 



• Pay — Start $6/hr; increase to $7-8/hr after 3 months. 



• Standout Candidates — For exceptional candidates, we will pay more than $8/hr 



• Growth — Opportunities to advance to senior SDR, team lead, and management



? What You'll Actually Do ?

• Make outbound B2B calls — Contact targeted prospects to book meetings

• Qualify prospects — Ask questions, pitch value quickly, and handle objections

• Maintain CRM records — Keep clean notes in HubSpot or Salesforce

• Share insights — Report what's working in daily team check-ins



? Must Have ?

• Excellent English — Clear and confident with minimal accent

• Experience in at least ONE of the following — telemarketing, inside sales, appointment setting, or other outbound calling or sales roles

• Quick learner — Comfortable learning and discussing technology topics for prospects

• Home office — Quiet workspace with reliable internet, computer, and headset



? Nice to Have ?

• Tech industry experience — Prior SDR work for tech, SaaS, or IT services

• Experience — Previous B2B appointment setting experience

• CRM familiarity — Experience with Salesforce or similar dialers

• Research skills — Proficiency with LinkedIn mining

• US hours experience — Comfortable working night shifts

• Team collaboration tools — Familiarity with Slack and Google Workspace

title: Client Onboarding Specialist (Payment Processing, Merchant Services, FinTech)
description:
We’re Hiring: Client Onboarding Specialist (Payment Processing, Merchant Services, Fintech) — VA

At Coastal Pay, we’re seeking a highly organized and detail-driven Client Onboarding Specialist to manage the full merchant activation process from contract to live processing.

This role is best suited for someone with direct experience in payment processing, merchant services, or fintech onboarding who can confidently guide merchants through compliance, technical setup, and go-live.

If you’ve worked with payment gateways, underwriting/KYC, or merchant account setup and enjoy bringing structure and clarity to complex processes, this role is a strong fit.



Role Summary

You will own the onboarding lifecycle for new merchants, ensuring they are approved, configured, and live as quickly and accurately as possible. This includes coordinating with underwriting, setting up gateway configurations, validating documentation, and troubleshooting issues before they delay activation.

You will act as both a project manager and merchant-facing guide, keeping onboarding on track while delivering a smooth, professional experience.



Schedule

100% Remote 

8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PT (required)

Must be comfortable working independently and staying highly responsive during U.S. business hours



What You’ll Do

Manage end-to-end onboarding for new merchants: application intake, document collection, underwriting follow-ups, and account setup

Guide merchants through onboarding requirements, ensuring all documentation is complete and accurate (business details, ownership, processing history)

Configure and validate payment gateway settings, API credentials, and integrations (POS, virtual terminals, etc.)

Coordinate closely with underwriting, risk, integrations, and operations teams to resolve issues and accelerate approvals

Support merchants through compliance requirements (PCI, KYC/AML basics) and ensure readiness for processing

Run test transactions and confirm successful setup before go-live

Troubleshoot onboarding issues such as declines, routing errors, or settlement configuration problems

Maintain accurate records and status updates in CRM and ticketing systems (Asana, Zendesk, Salesforce, etc.)

Communicate clearly with merchants and internal teams regarding timelines, requirements, and next steps

Identify process inefficiencies and contribute to improving onboarding workflows



What We’re Looking For

1+ years experience preferred in payments, merchant services, fintech, or gateway onboarding 

Strong understanding of merchant accounts, payment gateways, and transaction flow (card + ACH)

Familiarity with KYC, underwriting processes, and basic PCI compliance

Comfortable working with technical systems (gateways, APIs, dashboards)

Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal) — clear, professional, and merchant-facing

Highly organized with strong attention to detail

Able to manage multiple onboarding cases simultaneously without losing accuracy

Proactive problem-solver who takes ownership and follows issues through to completion

Reliable internet connection and dedicated remote workspace



Nice to Have (Strong Advantage)

Experience with platforms such as Stripe, Payrix, Adyen, Braintree, TSYS, or Fiserv

Prior experience working for an ISO, PSP, acquiring bank, or fintech company

Familiarity with CRM systems like Salesforce, HubSpot, IRIS, or similar

Experience handling chargebacks, risk reviews, or payment reconciliation



What We Offer

Competitive compensation (DOE)

Long-term, stable remote role with a growing fintech company

Supportive, collaborative team environment

Opportunity for professional growth

etings and execute action steps

 marketing campaigns

CORE EXPERTISE

Executive Calendar &
 Management

Lead Management & Strategic Pitching

Project Management & Task Coordination

Event Management & Marketing Support

Data Entry & Online Research

Systems Organization & Documentation

Partnership Relationship Management



BONUS EXPERTISE (PREFFERED)

Candidates with documented results (placements secured, contracts closed, partnerships developed) will be prioritized.



WORK EXPECTATIONS

COMPENSATION & PERFORMANCE STRUCTURE

APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS

s for Manifesting Serenity and 3–5 aligned podcasts currently seeking guests

 Protection Exercise: Below is a simulated inbox snapshot. Draft responses and explain what you would escalate, delegate, or decline.

If you are confident in your ability to operate at an executive support level, I look forward to reviewing your application.



Best,

Serenity L.
